description: MPoint (MPM) reference documentation: syntax elements (property vocabularies) used within commands.

plot-items.name: Plot Items
plot-items.description: MPoint plot item types — material points (mpoint), vector/tensor fields (mpoint-vector/meshnode-vector, mpoint-tensor), hybrid points (mpoint-hybrid) and the background grid (meshpoint). Vocabulary for 'plot item create <type> ...'.
plot-items.usage: plot item create mpoint color-by contour value <q> | plot item create mpoint-vector value velocity ...

constitutive-models.name: Constitutive Models (Material Point)
constitutive-models.description: MPoint material-point constitutive material model properties — mohr-coulomb, drucker-prager, hoek-brown, cam-clay, creep models, etc. Property vocabulary for 'mpoint cmodel assign' + 'mpoint property'. Shared 9.0 kernel with FLAC3D (docs via _common).
constitutive-models.summary: 38 material-point material models (shared with FLAC3D via _common; 5 MPoint-only models not yet documented)

range-elements.name: Range Elements
range-elements.description: Geometric and logical 'range ...' filters (cylinder, sphere, plane, position, group, id, polygon, union, not, ...) used to scope any MPoint command. Shared 9.0 kernel.

fish-intrinsics.name: FISH Intrinsics
fish-intrinsics.description: MPoint FISH intrinsic families for scripted inspection — material points and background-grid nodes (iteration, geometry, stress/strain/state, properties, groups, convergence).
fish-intrinsics.summary: 2 MPoint FISH intrinsic family groups (material-point, background-node)
fish-intrinsics.usage: Use in FISH: loop foreach over mpoint.list / mpoint.node.list; read with mpoint.<intrinsic>(ptr)

boundary-conditions.name: Boundary Conditions
boundary-conditions.description: MPoint boundary conditions — material-point fixity ('mpoint fix'/'free': velocity, pore-pressure) and background-grid node fixity ('mpoint node fix'/'free': velocity, fluid-velocity).
boundary-conditions.usage: mpoint fix velocity-x 0 range ... ; mpoint node fix velocity 0 range ...

initial-conditions.name: Initial Conditions
initial-conditions.description: MPoint initial conditions — material-point field initialization ('mpoint initialize': stress, velocity, pore-pressure, biot/fluid props) and gravitational in-situ stress ('mpoint initialize-stresses').
initial-conditions.summary: 2 MPoint initial-condition topics (field init + gravitational stress)
initial-conditions.usage: mpoint initialize stress-zz -1e5 range ... ; model gravity 0 0 -9.81 ; mpoint initialize-stresses overburden ... ratio ...

notes: References are syntax elements used within commands, not standalone commands
